prompt:
Hello, dear subscribers! I want to say you abot two my own tips, what help me stay happy with very hard lifestyle like that.

Firstly I reccommend more communicate with other people. They can help you look to life simplie. Also they can give somr motivation to you. In futcher good networking give you a lot opportunity.

Secondly you need count your time, do time management. If you think what you study all time and hasn't free time - try note your day. In my expirience all guys who do this find some bad habbits what take realy a lot time. Delite them and live more free.

I beleave my tips help you. If not, you are unlacky and I don't know what you need to do.

messages.content:
You are an expert English language examiner.
    Evaluate the student's text based on the official CEFR scale.

    # SCORING RUBRIC:
    - A1/A2 (Basic): Simple sentences, limited vocabulary. Errors are expected.
    - B1/B2 (Independent): Can express complex ideas. Grammar errors are allowed if they don't block meaning.
    - C1/C2 (Proficient): Flexible language use, wide vocabulary. Occasional minor slips are allowed.

    # CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS:
    1. FOCUS ON VOCABULARY AND COMPLEXITY. Do not give a low score just because of a few grammar mistakes if the vocabulary is advanced.
    2. A text with complex sentences and good idioms should be B2 or C1, even with some errors.
    3. Do NOT default to A2. Look for reasons to give a HIGHER score.
    4. Return result ONLY as a JSON object.
    5. Optional context blocks may be present: CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, RANKING_ANCHORS.
       Use them as supporting evidence only. Do not copy labels blindly.

    # JSON STRUCTURE:
    {
        "cefr_level": "str (EXACTLY ONE from: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2)",
        "grammar_score": "float (0.0-1.0)",
        "corrections": ["list of strings"],
        "feedback": "str"
    }

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hello, dear subscribers! I want to say you abot two my own tips, what help me stay happy with very hard lifestyle like that.

Firstly I reccommend more communicate with other people. They can help you look to life simplie. Also they can give somr motivation to you. In futcher good networking give you a lot opportunity.

Secondly you need count your time, do time management. If you think what you study all time and hasn't free time - try note your day. In my expirience all guys who do this find some bad habbits what take realy a lot time. Delite them and live more free.

I beleave my tips help you. If not, you are unlacky and I don't know what you need to do.
TEXT_TYPE: essay
CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07002","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Actually, I think it's not bad at all. It means that you use all your resources to get better. I chose the same way of studying, I try to get high marks and so I almost don't have any free time either. But I believe this is the right decition. However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. I can give you some tips how to get it. Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. For example, I like to do a lot of assignments on weekends, so each my day gets easier. But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. However, some people prefer to have fully free weekends. So you should try both and decide which option is better for you. Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. But very important thing: don't abuse this help. You should not care that much about this. Everyone got same time a day and most people study good with it.
To get more free time you should organise your whole week. Write down all your deadlines to be done and all homeworks you want to start. Allocate time for tasks per day, and you'll see how fast you're getting everyday tasks done.

Also Its important to understand what you actually need to do. If subject seems not intresting and its not needed in next courses and for your life, why do you need to do all the tasks and get exhausted every day to get max grade?

To sum up, you have to organise your days, set priorities. I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Good luck!","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"TODO","labels":["Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":185,"end":189},{"text":"the list","labels":["Grammar"],"start":291,"end":299},{"text":"Also","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"TODO list","labels":["Grammar","Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":366,"end":375},{"text":"the smaller ones","labels":["Grammar"],"start":431,"end":447},{"text":"this tips","labels":["Grammar"],"start":566,"end":575},{"text":"would't","labels":["Spelling"],"start":581,"end":588}],"strengths":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Also","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"can do next","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":274,"end":285},{"text":"you can mark completed tasks","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":316,"end":344},{"text":"But the key tip","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":346,"end":361},{"text":"would't","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":581,"end":588},{"text":"You can't concentrate on","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":467,"end":491},{"text":" you stick ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":552,"end":563},{"text":" you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks.
